nodes.label: Tests for ``tessera.compiler.jit_bridge`` — the Python → manifest → shared-loade
Each test gets a clean slate — no leaked trace rows, no     JIT-context leftover
Stack must unwind even if the inner block raises.
``tessera.ga.inner`` dispatches through the bridge and the     trace records one
Inside ``jit_context`` the trace row carries context=jit:apple_gpu.
The route is recorded differently but the numerical output is     bit-identical
``ga.inner`` still dispatches through the bridge when tracing is     off — the b
``JitBridgeMiss`` is raised when the manifest has no     ``apple_gpu=fused`` ent
Ops whose only manifest entry is ``planned`` (no fused symbol)     raise :class:
Routes are immutable — callers can stash them safely.
